text.title: backup-database-postgres Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backup-database-postgres as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates pg_dump script without specifying -Fc (custom format), producing a plain SQL file that cannot be partially restored or used with pg_restore -j for parallel restore.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plain SQL format is human-readable but cannot do parallel restore or selective table restore. Always use -Fc or -Fd for production backups.

failure-mode.description: Agent sets up WAL archiving with archive_command pointing to a path without verifying the archive_status in pg_stat_archiver after enabling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: archive_command failures are silent from the application's perspective; the database continues to run while the PITR chain silently breaks. Verify after every archive_command change.

failure-mode.description: Agent commits Restic password or PGPASSWORD into the backup script or repository instead of loading from secrets manager.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Encryption key loss = data loss; credential exposure in Git = security incident. Force pull from secrets manager and add a git pre-commit check: `git diff --staged | grep -E 'RESTIC_PASSWORD|PGPASSWORD'`.
